status: VALID. Supersedes the schema-v1 record of the same tag, which reported a prefill/extend pass as 92.9% decode 'attention' and is retained only in git history.
intent: Profile Laguna's native block-FP8 path at depth to find the limiting kernels, comparable to the 2026-07-18 auto-path categories.

served_identity.model_geometry: 40 layers = 10 full_attention + 30 sliding_attention (window 512); 48 q heads, 8 kv heads, head_dim 128

method.phase_separation: Kernel time is attributed to a phase as well as a category. The phase boundary is the first decode-attention kernel per rank. Phase membership is decided by EXACT kernel name: _fwd_kernel and _fwd_kernel_unified are extend/prefill (extend_attention.py, prefill_attention.py); _fwd_kernel_stage1/2 and _fwd_grouped_kernel_stage1 are decode (decode_attention.py). A substring test for '_fwd_kernel' matches all of them, which is what merged the phases in the superseded run.
method.step_counting: Traced decode steps are derived from stage-1 decode-attention calls (once per layer per step; stage2 would double count), not from the requested step count.
method.arms: Two independent runs: production config (CUDA graphs ON) and graphs OFF. Graphs ON traces only ~1 decode step because graph replays are not recorded kernel-by-kernel; graphs OFF traces 39 of 40 but distorts collectives.

decode_phase.note: Percentages are of traced GPU time within the decode phase only.

graphs_off_eager.caveat: The 52.2% rccl is an ARTIFACT of disabling CUDA graphs: eager launches desynchronize the two ranks and the collective kernel spends its time spin-waiting for its peer. Not a production decode characteristic.

agreement_excluding_collectives.note: With rccl removed, the two arms agree on the ordering, which is the robust result.

decode_phase.finding: Decode on the native FP8 path is dominated by dense rocBLAS/Tensile GEMM (~38-42% of non-collective GPU time), NOT by attention. Attention is second at ~20-28%.
decode_phase.open_question: rocblas_gemm remains the largest non-collective decode category even with fp8_gemm_runner_backend=triton, while triton_fp8_gemm is only ~7-12%. Which dense GEMMs still route to rocBLAS on this checkpoint is NOT established here and is the next lead.

prefill_extend_phase.shape: The profiled request appended 1 new token to a 197,193-token cache hit, verified in the server log as '#new-token: 1, #cached-token: 197193'. This is the agentic tool-result shape.
prefill_extend_phase.per_layer_structure: _fwd_kernel fired 40 times per rank -- once per layer, ONE extend forward pass. Exactly 10 calls cost ~35 ms each and 30 cost ~130-350 us. That maps one-to-one onto the checkpoint's 10 full_attention and 30 sliding_attention (window 512) layers: the cost is entirely the full-attention layers walking the whole cached prefix.
prefill_extend_phase.comparison: Extend attention ~361 ms/rank versus decode attention ~4.2 ms/rank over the SAME KV cache -- an ~85x gap on identical data. The ratio is independent of any memory-bandwidth assumption because both phases read the same bytes.

mechanism.claim: The extend kernel does not split the KV dimension, so a short query gets almost no parallelism.
evidence: decode_attention.py -- grid = (batch_size, cdiv(head_num, min(BLOCK_H, kv_group_num)), num_kv_splits)
On gfx1201 with head_dim 128, _get_block_sizes_for_extend_attention returns BLOCK_M=64.

For a cache-hit turn max_len_extend is the SUFFIX length, so cdiv(1,64) = cdiv(64,64) = 1: the extend grid's third dimension collapses to 1 and, with batch 1 and 24 heads per rank, ~24 workgroups walk the entire prefix. Decode splits the same walk 64 ways.
The upstream source comment at extend_attention.py:37 states 'each workgroup reads the whole prefix'.
mechanism.falsifiable_prediction_and_result: Because cdiv(1,64) == cdiv(64,64), appending 1 token and appending 64 tokens must cost the SAME wall time. Measured at 176,588 cached tokens: 604.6 ms versus 607.7 ms, a 0.5% difference for 64x the tokens. Prediction held at every depth (8K: 61.3/68.3; 32K: 123.3/136.3; 131K: 409.3/422.3).

user_visible_cost.method: Streaming TTFT of a cache-hit request appending N new tokens to a primed prefix, median of 3 runs, each run with a unique suffix. Cache hits verified via --enable-cache-report.
user_visible_cost.linear_fit: TTFT_ms ~= 32.7 + 3.226 per 1000 cached tokens; predicts all four measured depths within 8%.
user_visible_cost.extrapolation: ~878 ms per agentic turn at the 262,144 context limit. NOT measured -- extrapolated from the fit.
user_visible_cost.contrast: Over the same 7.4K -> 176.6K range, extend cost grew 9.86x while decode TPOT grew only 1.23x (14.32 -> 17.61 ms). Decode scales well with depth; extend does not.
user_visible_cost.in_user_terms: At 176,588 tokens an agentic tool-result turn waits 605 ms before its first token -- the time of 34 decode tokens -- and a realistic 64-token tool result costs the same 605 ms.

not_established: Which dense GEMMs still route to rocBLAS rather than the native triton FP8 path during decode.

The achievable win from splitting KV in extend, or from routing short-suffix extends to the decode kernel. The mechanism is identified and the headroom is large, but no fix has been implemented or measured.
Whether the same extend grid collapse dominates North-Mini, which is a different architecture (Cohere2MoE, hybrid-SWA, 49 layers). Only Laguna was profiled.
reproduce: EXTRA_ARGS=--enable-cache-report scripts/launch.sh laguna, then python scripts/bench/measure_extend_cost.py --port 23334 --depths 8192,32768,131072,197000 --suffix-tokens 1,64,512 --runs 3
